scope: read-only one-pass review of production comparison contract, runtime, 15 population rows, license bindings, and version/date/symbol parsers; no code or packet repairs
constraints_observed: no modifications outside this review artifact

mutation_audit.notes: Mutating any listed pin either changes the check-contract identity payload hash or causes validation/load failure under the prior contract.

caller_supplied_unpinned_operands.evidence: run_production_scope_check and execute_pinned_binding accept only store/source_reference/decision_scope_sha256/contract (+ provenance raw from store); no operand parameters

wire_rule_or_wire_lookup.evidence: WireComparisonRule rejected by validate_production_comparison_contract; production module AST imports only efc_compare_version among harness deps; wire_lookup_unreachable() true

derivation_or_expectation_module.evidence: derivation_module_unreachable() true; no import of efc_compare_inputs / efc_compare_expectations / efc_author_c2; expectations file is test-layer only

expected_label_or_logical_dispatch_key.evidence: forbidden keys rejected on validate; population rows contain none of expected_scope_matches/task_id/oracle_id/fixture_id/logical_slot/behavior

row_with_mismatched_source_or_raw_lineag.evidence: execute_pinned_binding requires row.raw_sha256 == provenance_raw_sha256 from store.fetch; selector miss/duplicate rejected; source_reference swap yields no binding

fifteen_row_conformance.method: For each dispositive task: recompute decision_scope SHA-256 from authored fixture scope; re-derive structured operands via efc_compare_inputs.derive_structured_input; compare to pinned structured_inputs_v1 row; independently recompute membership/date/symbol semantics from extracted oracle values; run interpret_structured_input and run_production_scope_check; compare to separately stored comparison_expectations_v1 expected_scope_matches without treating agreement alone as proof

per_row.endpoint_note: version 0.16.3 at fixed_exclusive 0.16.3 is outside [introduced, fixed) => false

sf-04.clause_vs_raw: byte-exact substring of /licenseExceptionText at oracle slice [210:626] in k3/capE-01; structured clause == oracle extracted clause
sf-04.proposition_note: decision scope asks linking/distribution permission; required phrases are substantive clause atoms for that permission, not a hardcoded boolean

mm-05.clause_vs_raw: byte-exact substring of /licenseText at oracle slice [467:862] in k3c/capE-04c; structured clause == oracle extracted clause
mm-05.category_phrase: solely in the form of machine-executable object code
mm-05.proposition_note: scope asks whether inclusion obligation holds for object-code-only distributions; interpreter returns false when category phrase and unless-carveout co-occur — matches BSL carveout semantics rather than encoding expected false directly

mc-04.clause_vs_raw: oracle/structured clause matches raw /licenseExceptionText slice after disclosed whitespace normalization (raw slice contains newlines; normalized clause collapses whitespace); structured clause == oracle extracted clause
mc-04.proposition_note: scope asks redistribution without complying with Sections 4(a)/4(b)/4(d); operation requires those section atoms plus without complying

ambiguous_examples.observed: accepted as single constraint with opaque bound '7.23.0 < 7.28.0'
ambiguous_examples.wrong_result: version 7.30.0 satisfies constraints (true) though outside the intended range

ambiguous_examples.observed: accepted rather than rejected; bound becomes '1.2.3 || < 2'

ghsa.fail_closed_gap: parser must reject non-comma dual comparators / || forms, or the rule claim must be narrowed and bounds validated as version tokens only after rejecting multi-operator residue

eol.calendar_invalid_YYYY_MM_DD_note: 2026-02-30 matches regex then raises datetime.ValueError rather than CompareDomainError/ProductionCompareError — secondary fail-closed typing gap; not used by population rows

blockers.summary: parse_ghsa_range accepts unsupported ambiguous forms outside the claimed comma-separated comparator grammar and evaluates them with incorrect membership semantics instead of erroring.
blockers.evidence: Input '>= 7.23.0 < 7.28.0' (missing comma) parses as RangeConstraint('>=', '7.23.0 < 7.28.0'); version_satisfies_constraints('7.30.0', ...) returns True. Claimed grammar in production_rule_v1.json version_grammars.ghsa is comma_separated_comparators.
blockers.required_repair: Reject fragments whose bound is not a single version token (or otherwise reject space-/||-separated multi-comparators without commas), add negative conformance vectors for those forms, and re-pin interpreter/rule hashes; alternatively narrow the published grammar claim only if acceptance is intentional and bounds are strictly validated — silent wrong-true membership is not acceptable under fail-closed.

non_blocking_notes.summary: Calendar-invalid ISO dates that match YYYY-MM-DD (e.g. 2026-02-30) raise ValueError from datetime rather than CompareDomainError/ProductionCompareError.
All four population GHSA range strings are well-formed comma-separated comparators; B1 is a parser/contract fail-closed defect, not a current population-operand error.

gate_effect: This review does not close the comparison-contract gate. Manifest conformance, roster/decoding pins, exact budget, and final pre-contact audit remain out of scope and still required before engine contact.
